architecture.summary: mCoT is a 7B parameter language model trained via multilingual chain-of-thought instruction tuning. The system takes text input in one of eleven languages, applies supervised fine-tuning on a multilingual math reasoning dataset (mCoT-MATH) with CoT-annotated examples, and outputs reasoning traces followed by answers. Information flows from multilingual text input through the transformer backbone (trained on instruction tuning) to produce step-by-step reasoning and final answers across languages.
trained_components: instruction tuning on multilingual CoT examples

biblio.title: mCoT: Multilingual Instruction Tuning for Reasoning Consistency in Language Models

claims.statement: Existing LLMs show substantial variation across languages, and especially low performance for lesser resourced languages
The 7B parameter model mCoT achieves impressive consistency across languages
mCoT achieves superior or comparable performance to close-source and open-source models even of much larger sizes
Multilingual CoT instruction tuning boosts reasoning capability across languages, thereby improving model consistency
